text:
Once upon a time, there was a quaint gingerbread house nestled between two old oak trees. The house had three windows, one door, and a roof shaped like a cat's nose. Every autumn, it shimmered in the golden leaves, and birds would visit just to peck at the sugar feathers sticking out.

Now, there lived an enormous, wise owl named Olga. She was feathered all shades of wisdom—gray fur with blue eyes that seemed to see right through you. Every dawn, Olga would roam the forest. One particular morning, she decided to venture closer to town.

After some time, she settled gracefully into her favorite tree overlooking the street. The town seemed sleepy compared to the forest. Everyone paused their morning activities to admire her shadow. Olga hooted warmly, a soft and melodious sound that hinted at centuries of quiet wisdom.

That morning, though, something peculiar caught her attention. A small brown squirrel dived towards her nestful of acorn
